mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising oil prices signal supply-side bottlenecks that increase inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when both INDPRO declines and CPI rises above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1,000 units signals weakening demand for construction, reducing corporate revenue expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 1,000.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) above $450 billion signals strong demand for goods, increasing corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 450 billion.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 2.5% signals central bank tightening, increasing equity discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where CPI exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment below 4.5% signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts signals strong economic demand, increasing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth.
